relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: A teaching arrives that makes circumcision necessary for saving/healing.
The dispute becomes important enough for Jerusalem discernment.
The nations’ turning brings joy through Phoenicia and Samaria.
Jerusalem receives the report of what the Divine has done.
Some trusted Pharisees argue for circumcision and Moses-observance.
Apostles and elders gather around the question.
Peter recalls the Divine choice that nations hear and trust.
The heart-knowing Divine witnessed by giving holy Spirit.
No distinction remains where hearts are cleansed by trust.
The yoke is named as too heavy to place on the nations.
Grace saves/heals both us and them in the same way.
The assembly hears signs and wonders among the nations.
James asks the siblings to hear.
Simeon’s story is a visitation to take a people from the nations for the name.
The prophets agree with this widening.
David’s fallen tent will be rebuilt.
The restored tent opens seeking to the remnant and all name-called nations.
This is known from the age.
James judges not to trouble the nations turning to the Divine.
A minimal table-fellowship boundary is offered.
Moses is still read every Sabbath in every city.
The whole assembly chooses messengers with Paul and Barnabas.
The letter greets Gentile siblings as siblings.
Unauthorized troubling words are corrected.
One accord chooses trusted messengers.
Barnabas and Paul have risked their lives for the name.
Judas and Silas will confirm the letter by word.
Discernment is shared: it seemed good to holy Spirit and to us.
Necessary abstentions are given for beautiful walking together.
The letter is delivered to Antioch.
The community rejoices over encouragement.
Prophetic words strengthen the siblings.
They are released in peace.
A later manuscript tradition keeps Silas in Antioch.
Paul and Barnabas remain teaching good news with many others.
Paul desires to revisit and strengthen the siblings.
Barnabas wants Mark included.
Paul judges Mark’s earlier withdrawal as disqualifying for this work.
A sharp disagreement separates two mission paths.
Paul and Silas are handed to grace by the siblings.
The assemblies in Syria and Cilicia are strengthened.
